violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in the walk-in cooler with food. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

An employee was observed scooping hashbrowns into service bags; hashbrowns touched employee's bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: -chest freezer and 1 door lowboy (out of service prior to inspection) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

Open employees' beverage containers were observed stored in a walk-in cooler. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Milk, orange juice and light cream were observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Establishment was not maintaining proper labelling for Time as a Public Health Control for tomatoes, onions, and lettuce. PIC informed and labels were marked during time of inspection. Permission to use Time as a Public Health control is contingent upon active managerial control. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the 1 door lowboy refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: -chest freezer (out of service prior to inspection) -1 door lowboy (ambient temperature of 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit) New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. -shelving in walk-in cooler -hot holding unit frames (interior) -behind frozen drink machine -ice machine -exterior of various equipment New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

The following has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: -floors under shelving in walk-in cooler. -walk-in cooler fan guards and surrounding ceiling. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machines were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off and ice was voluntarily disposed of. Equipment to be properly cleaned and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. 1 door lowboy (out of service prior to inspection) 2. 1 door lowboy gaskets 3. walk-in freezer gaskets Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. ice cream machine 2. hot holding units 3. 3 bay faucets 4. various counter tops New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The floors underneath equipment in the kitchen and walk-in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1. grout by 3 bay sink, mop sink, and makeline drain 2. 3 bay faucets (observed leaking) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
